sectionName: Biological & Biochemical Foundations of Living Systems
topicArea: Immune Tolerance, T Cell Activation & Transplantation

questions.passage: Immunologists activated naive T cells using plate-bound anti-CD3 antibody, which stimulates the T cell receptor complex. Some wells also contained B7-expressing antigen-presenting cells. In a third condition, CTLA4-Ig was added to bind B7 and prevent CD28 costimulation. The figure shows IL-2 secretion, proliferation, and anergy markers.
questions.stem: Which condition produced the strongest T cell activation?

choices.C: No TCR stimulation with CTLA4-Ig only

explanations.A: TCR signal without costimulation leads to weak activation and anergy markers.
explanations.B: CTLA4-Ig blocks B7 costimulation, reducing activation.
explanations.C: Without TCR signal, T cells are not specifically activated.
explanations.D: Correct. T cells require TCR stimulation plus costimulation through CD28-B7 for strong IL-2 production and proliferation.

questions.passageImageCaption: Figure 1. T cell responses after TCR stimulation with or without costimulation.

questions.stem: What is the main effect of CTLA4-Ig in this experiment?

choices.A: It prevents B7 from activating CD28 on T cells.
choices.B: It degrades peptide-MHC complexes inside the nucleus
choices.C: It directly opens voltage-gated Na+ channels
choices.D: It converts naive T cells into erythrocytes

explanations.A: Correct. CTLA4-Ig binds B7 molecules and blocks the costimulatory signal needed for full T cell activation.
explanations.B: CTLA4-Ig acts extracellularly on costimulation, not nuclear peptide-MHC degradation.
explanations.C: CTLA4-Ig is not an ion-channel agonist.
explanations.D: T cells do not differentiate into erythrocytes.

questions.stem: Why do anergy markers rise when T cells receive anti-CD3 alone?

choices.A: Anti-CD3 alone fully activates all cytokine pathways
choices.B: B7 directly kills all T cells in the anti-CD3-only condition
choices.C: TCR signaling without adequate costimulation can induce functional unresponsiveness.
choices.D: Anergy is caused by antibody binding to hemoglobin

explanations.A: The figure shows weak IL-2 and proliferation, not full activation.
explanations.B: B7 is absent in that condition.
explanations.C: Correct. Signal 1 without signal 2 is a classic mechanism promoting peripheral tolerance/anergy.
explanations.D: Hemoglobin is unrelated to T cell tolerance.

questions.stem: Which molecule is part of the TCR coreceptor complex on helper T cells?

explanations.A: CD8 is typical of cytotoxic T cells and binds MHC class I.
explanations.B: Correct. Helper T cells express CD4, which interacts with MHC class II.
explanations.C: IgE receptors are associated with mast cells/basophils, not helper T cell coreceptor function.
explanations.D: Glycophorin A is an erythrocyte membrane protein.

questions.stem: IL-2 most directly supports T cell activation by promoting:

choices.A: Clonal expansion of activated T cells
choices.D: Repression of the bacterial lac operon

explanations.A: Correct. IL-2 is a T cell growth cytokine that drives proliferation after activation.
explanations.B: Hemoglobin handles oxygen binding, not IL-2.
explanations.C: Collagen digestion is not the primary IL-2 function.
explanations.D: IL-2 does not regulate bacterial operons.

questions.passage: A transplant team compared graft outcomes in three mouse groups: MHC-mismatched grafts without immunosuppression, MHC-mismatched grafts treated with tacrolimus, and MHC-matched grafts. Tacrolimus inhibits calcineurin-dependent T cell activation. Rejection markers were measured over nine days.
questions.stem: The high rejection marker levels in untreated MHC-mismatched grafts are most directly due to:

choices.A: Recipient red blood cells binding oxygen too tightly
choices.B: Failure of donor cells to perform glycolysis
choices.C: Loss of bacterial plasmids in donor tissue
choices.D: Recognition of nonself MHC molecules by recipient T cells

explanations.A: Oxygen affinity is not the main driver of allograft rejection.
explanations.B: The figure compares immune compatibility and tacrolimus, not glycolytic capacity.
explanations.C: Transplants involve mammalian tissues, not bacterial conjugation.
explanations.D: Correct. MHC mismatch strongly activates alloreactive T cells, leading to graft rejection.

questions.passageImageCaption: Figure 2. Relative rejection markers after transplant under different compatibility and treatment conditions.

questions.stem: Tacrolimus most directly suppresses T cell activation by reducing transcription of which cytokine?

explanations.A: EPO regulates red blood cell production, not the main T cell activation loop.
explanations.B: Insulin controls glucose metabolism and is not the key calcineurin-dependent T cell cytokine.
explanations.C: Correct. Calcineurin signaling promotes NFAT-dependent IL-2 transcription; tacrolimus inhibits this pathway.
explanations.D: hCG maintains the corpus luteum in pregnancy and is unrelated to tacrolimus action.

questions.stem: Which cells would directly kill donor cells displaying foreign peptide-MHC I complexes?

choices.A: Naive B cells secreting only IgM without activation

explanations.A: B cells can make antibodies but do not directly kill MHC I-bearing cells this way.
explanations.B: Correct. CD8+ T cells recognize antigen on MHC I and can kill target cells through perforin/granzyme pathways.
explanations.C: Eosinophils specialize in parasite/allergic responses, not direct allograft cytotoxicity.
explanations.D: Platelets aid clotting and do not perform antigen-specific cytotoxicity.

questions.stem: Why does MHC matching reduce rejection marker levels?

choices.A: It prevents all donor cells from expressing proteins
choices.B: It converts donor T cells into recipient erythrocytes
choices.C: It raises tacrolimus concentration without giving drug
choices.D: It lowers the likelihood that recipient lymphocytes recognize graft cells as foreign.

explanations.A: MHC matching does not stop protein expression.
explanations.B: Cell identity is not converted by MHC matching.
explanations.C: MHC matching and drug treatment are separate conditions.
explanations.D: Correct. Closer MHC similarity reduces alloreactive T cell activation.

questions.stem: Which experimental measurement would best confirm reduced T cell activation in the tacrolimus group?

choices.A: Lower nuclear NFAT target gene expression
choices.C: Increased fetal hemoglobin expression in graft tissue

explanations.A: Correct. Tacrolimus inhibits calcineurin/NFAT signaling, so NFAT-dependent transcription should decrease.
explanations.B: Bacterial lacZ expression is unrelated to mouse graft T cell activation.
explanations.C: Hemoglobin type is not a direct T cell activation marker.
explanations.D: Actin polymerization is not the most specific readout of calcineurin inhibition.
